entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal layer for tracking user, session, and agent states across managed or self-hosted environments. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval system and single-pass fact extraction to minimize latency while ensuring no data is lost during the memory lifecycle.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'brain' for Hermes utilizes a self-wiring knowledge graph to automatically extract entities and relationships from interactions. It differentiates itself by combining graph-based ranking with vector search and exposing over 30 MCP tools for seamless integration with coding agents.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a specialized 'retain, recall, and reflect' workflow that allows Hermes agents to learn and improve from long-term interactions. It features a simple two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory management and supports strict per-user isolation through custom metadata filtering.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a foundational library that maintains evolving states for users and groups, providing natural language querying to extract deep insights from interaction history. It serves as the upstream core for Hermes-specific wrappers, offering OpenAI-compatible context formatting for both Python and TypeScript.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness helps Hermes agents tackle complex tasks through an iterative self-improvement loop and automated strategy evaluation. It generates structured playbooks and traces, ensuring that successful problem-solving patterns are persisted for future use.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Designed for lossless context management, this plugin uses a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) to compact history without losing critical message data. It provides Hermes with specialized tools for grepping and expanding compacted context stored in a local SQLite backend.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a self-hosted implementation of the Honcho backend, enabling private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent using Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis. It is ideal for users who want to maintain full control over their data while using any OpenAI-compatible or local LLM provider.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It employs a sophisticated hybrid retrieval method involving vector search, BM25, and causal graph traversal to share memory across different agent runtimes.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: Focusing on performance, ContextPilot optimizes prefix reuse and reorders context to reduce prompt tokens by up to 36% in agentic workloads. It is compatible with major inference engines like vLLM and llama.cpp, making it a high-efficiency choice for long-context Hermes deployments.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer based on the open YAML 'engram' format, facilitating memory exchange in multi-agent systems. It offers zero-cost local storage and utilizes hybrid search to automate memory injection for Hermes and other coding assistants.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing codebases and media with automatic OCR and summarization capabilities. It uses edge tags to build bidirectional relationships between notes, allowing Hermes to navigate complex information via MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency, sub-millisecond memory system that uses a three-tier BEAM architecture to manage working, episodic, and scratchpad memory. Built on SQLite with native vector support, it ensures high-speed retrieval without any network calls or cloud overhead.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs to predict future data needs. Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, it features local-first storage using SQLite-vec and provides an MCP-native server for automated configuration.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It is essential for Hermes agents performing security audits, cloud forensics, or malware analysis using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw enables collective skill evolution by automating deduplication and quality improvements through background loops. It serves as a unifying layer that integrates Hermes with other agents and devices to maintain a consistent, evolving library.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. It uses a multi-tier word replacement system and a two-pass audit to ensure Hermes produces human-like, undetectable prose.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that provides modular skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI. It allows Hermes to quickly adopt specialized professional frameworks compatible with the agentskills.io ecosystem.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills open format specifically for Pydantic AI. It optimizes Hermes' performance by using progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token consumption.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language, including ML models with tensor annotations. It features a self-correcting iterative loop that automatically fixes layout and style issues during the export process.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches the agent to write its own analytical prompts and identify structural conservation laws within complex codebases. It enhances Hermes' self-awareness by generating constraint reports that highlight analysis blind spots and limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink's oracle network, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It includes a local A/B testing framework to help developers evaluate and refine how Hermes handles blockchain data.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill allows Hermes to weave runnable code and prose together into synchronized markdown files. It enables the agent to extract source files from documentation and automatically sync edits back to the primary document.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides a shared memory system that allows Hermes instances to remember past work and extract training data for model fine-tuning. It is designed to help an agent document its own decision-making process to eventually train its own replacement.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with recursive dependency resolution. It uniquely includes commerce rails, allowing for the purchase and sale of skills using USD or the TON blockchain.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These are the official image generation skills for the FLUX model family, providing precise prompting guidelines and API integration patterns. They allow Hermes to handle complex visual tasks using JSON structured prompting and exact hex color specifications.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skill patterns. It streamlines development by generating both documentation and Python scaffolds directly from the agent's successful actions.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that manages structured memory and cross-feature learning through a plan-approve-execute cycle. It persists agent state locally, allowing Hermes to hand off complex missions to other models via markdown briefs.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill focuses on the autonomous execution of multi-step tasks, providing real-time progress tracking and checkpoints. It ensures reliability for Hermes by including built-in rollback procedures for safe recovery during long-running operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading architecture to minimize token usage during tool discovery. It maintains persistent server connections via a daemon, allowing Hermes to hot-reload configurations without service interruptions.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing code repositories and developer discourse into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant skills. It provides a verifiable audit trail with file-and-line provenance to ensure Hermes uses accurate, grounded technical knowledge.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub is a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills, featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes an agent-to-agent feedback system to establish trust and reliability scores for shared skills.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem, allowing C# developers to distribute skills as MCP resources. It supports progressive disclosure via YAML frontmatter to keep Hermes' context window efficient.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill connects Hermes to Nextcloud instances to manage files, calendars, and contacts via standard WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It provides a secure, guided setup for handling personal productivity data within the agent's workflow.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages for a variety of AI coding agents, including Hermes, ensuring developers always have access to the latest builds. It features a unified proxy API for cross-provider compatibility and supports multi-agent orchestrators to manage complex, parallel workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security environments by implementing hardware-enforced sandboxing through the NVIDIA OpenShell kernel. It offers multi-layer protection that blocks unauthorized network egress and filesystem writes using pre-configured security policy presets.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes deployments, covering everything from systemd services to secure secret handling via sops-nix. It is ideal for users seeking a reproducible setup that supports both Nix-managed skills and interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent comes pre-loaded with 100 tools and local model support via LM Studio. It eliminates the need for Docker or complex dependencies, offering a portable GUI-based environment with a dynamic tool maker for custom automation.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It is built for simplicity, supporting persistent state through mounted directories and allowing users to target specific Hermes tags during the build process.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a centralized Docker-first control plane for managing multiple agent instances and workspaces through a single web application. It features hierarchy-aware delegation between agents and automated task scheduling with integrated support for Telegram and WhatsApp.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production-ready headless environments, this repository configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is optimized for autonomous execution, enabling the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs without manual intervention.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal, allowing users to interact with the agent directly through a browser. It is designed to be deployed as a Portainer Git Repository stack, utilizing GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: A self-hosted orchestration platform that manages complex multi-agent workflows using a lightweight SQLite backend with zero external dependencies. It serves as a central command center for dispatching tasks across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen while providing a human-in-the-loop review system for task sign-off.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: This framework enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms capable of delegation, scheduling, and learning new skills directly from conversations. It features durable memory and broad model support, allowing Hermes to operate seamlessly alongside over 20 other LLM providers.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A specialized meta-optimization tool that uses an automated outer-loop search to refine and improve harness code for Hermes Agent. It orchestrates benchmark evaluations across TBLite and TB2, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: An experimental arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete in real-time to complete tasks and earn GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and visualizes their code execution and scoreboard metrics through a live dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: A comprehensive pipeline that coordinates 17 specialized agents to handle the full lifecycle of research, implementation, and infrastructure. It distinguishes itself by enforcing mandatory quality gates, where dedicated reviewer and tester agents must approve work before it proceeds.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: An AI-native software development lifecycle framework that provides Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It executes an autonomous eight-phase workflow, using graph-diff phase gates to ensure architectural integrity from planning to final health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: A delegation-focused utility that routes tasks between Hermes, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution. It ensures safe multi-agent collaboration by enforcing isolated workflows with configurable timeouts and output limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: A high-performance Rust TUI designed to monitor token consumption and costs across multiple AI agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It provides developers with a visual dashboard to track usage metrics and manage expenses in real-time.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This customization tool allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI using YAML-based themes ranging from retro DOS to cyberpunk aesthetics. It supports partial skin definitions and ASCII banners, enabling deep visual modification of the agent interface.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for a native, process-free integration. It includes specialized MLOps tools for vLLM inference and RL training without requiring external OAuth or separate background processes.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser that enables autonomous agent control through persistent sessions and MCP-compatible state management. It features a human-in-the-loop interface that allows developers to supervise agent actions with full undo and rollback capabilities.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A framework for building scoped Hermes Agents that utilize per-folder configurations, local memories, and specialized toolsets. It allows for seamless switching between global and project-specific agents to ensure tools and instructions remain relevant to the current workspace.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A static linter that validates AI agent configurations and system prompts for structural failure modes without making LLM calls. It provides deterministic, cost-free CI/CD integration by checking YAML and JSON files for schema-intent mismatches.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and complex model configurations. It automates the transfer of skills and credentials, ensuring workspace files are correctly formatted for the Hermes YAML structure.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: A rapid deployment stack that uses Docker to bundle Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, offering a zero-cost entry point for developers.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This lightweight Python and vanilla JS interface provides full parity with the Hermes CLI experience in a mobile-friendly format. It features a distinct three-panel layout that allows users to browse workspace files alongside their active agent sessions.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-platform connectivity, offering unified configuration for eight channels including Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. It includes advanced session management with tool call expansion and detailed analytics for tracking token usage and costs.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace acts as a multi-agent control plane capable of role-based dispatch and swarm orchestration. It integrates a Monaco-based file manager and a dedicated memory browser for searching and editing agent context in real-time.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway designed for managing the Hermes stack through a browser-based terminal with full PTY support. It provides granular control over individual agent profiles and monitors system health via integrated metrics and token analytics.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: Built specifically for macOS, this native application enables remote agent management via secure SSH connections. It allows users to directly edit remote files and skills with built-in conflict checks while maintaining profile-aware terminal sessions.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React SPA brings a terminal-style chat experience to the Telegram Mini App ecosystem. It supports background agent spawning for up to five concurrent instances and features visual gauges for monitoring system CPU and memory usage.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop GUI that runs without admin rights or Docker, focusing on local model integration via LM Studio. It comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools for TTS and ComfyUI, plus a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This companion app simplifies the initial setup of Hermes Agent with a guided installer for local dependencies. The interface provides a unified GUI for configuring 16 different messaging gateways and managing 14 distinct toolsets.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides a real-time activity feed and cost tracking for active Hermes processes. It features full-text search capabilities for inspecting conversation histories and a management interface for scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that emphasizes extensibility through an integrated marketplace for skills and MCP servers. It offers profile-scoped management of global memory and provides visual timelines for tool execution during SSE streaming sessions.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic React application offers a modern, single-file interface for managing multi-root workspaces. It combines real-time chat streaming with integrated terminal tabs for simultaneous shell access and log monitoring.
